pairs.question: What is the AP NFL Assistant Coach of the Year award and who presents it?
pairs.answer: The AP NFL Assistant Coach of the Year (ACOY) is an annual award given by the Associated Press to the top assistant coach in the National Football League (NFL). It recognizes excellence among non-head coaching staff in the NFL.

pairs.question: How is the winner of the AP NFL Assistant Coach of the Year award determined?
pairs.answer: The winner is selected through votes from a nationwide panel of sportswriters who regularly cover the NFL. These votes determine the top assistant coach based on their performance during the season.

pairs.question: When and where was the AP NFL Assistant Coach of the Year award first presented?
pairs.answer: The award was first presented following the 2014 NFL season at the 4th NFL Honors. It was introduced as the first new AP award in the NFL since the reintroduction of the Comeback Player of the Year award in 1998.

pairs.question: Who was the first recipient of the AP NFL Assistant Coach of the Year award and what happened after they won?
pairs.answer: Todd Bowles was the inaugural winner of the award in 2014. Following his award-winning season, he was hired as the head coach of the New York Jets.

pairs.question: Can head coaches win the AP NFL Assistant Coach of the Year award? Explain.
pairs.answer: No, head coaches are not eligible for the award. It is specifically designed to recognize assistant coaches, though historically, only offensive or defensive coordinators have won it.

pairs.question: Name two assistant coaches who won the AP NFL Assistant Coach of the Year award and later became head coaches.
pairs.answer: Vic Fangio and Kyle Shanahan are two notable examples. After winning the award, Fangio became the head coach of the Denver Broncos, and Shanahan became the head coach of the San Francisco 49ers.

pairs.question: What is notable about Josh McDaniels' win of the AP NFL Assistant Coach of the Year award?
pairs.answer: Josh McDaniels, the offensive coordinator for the 2025 New England Patriots, is the most recent winner of the award, having claimed the title in the latest season.
